nativeRange.habitat: Generally found growing emersed or submersed in seasonal wetlands, on the muddy beds of dried ponds and lakes, and in slow-moving freshwater systems across the Pantanal and Caribbean. The 'Cuba' and 'Pantanal' verticillate forms are the most widely known in the aquarium trade, though availability varies considerably — the 'Cuba' form tends to be the more accessible of the two in Western markets.

decorPreferences: midground to background placement as a colour-accent group

summary: Ludwigia inclinata is a highly prized red-and-yellow stem plant from the Pantanal wetlands and Cuba, best known through the verticillate 'Cuba' and 'Pantanal' forms — widely regarded as some of the most sought-after and demanding red plants in the planted-tank hobby.
careNotes: Ludwigia inclinata is a fragile herbaceous stem plant in the family Onagraceae, native to Central and South America with a particular concentration in the Pantanal wetlands of Brazil. In the aquarium trade it is most commonly encountered as one of several verticillate forms: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Cuba', 'Pantanal' (the most prized Brazilian form), 'Tornado' (a twisted-leaf variant), and 'Curly'. All share broadly similar husbandry requirements, though cultivar-specific colour intensity and leaf form vary — this entry covers the species-level baseline that applies across them. Lighting requirements are high, typically around 0.5–1 W/L of full-spectrum LED or an equivalent PAR output. CO2 injection is strongly recommended, and heavy iron supplementation combined with a complete micronutrient regime is needed to develop the trademark red-to-yellow gradient on the upper leaves. Growth is fast when conditions are stable, but the plant is intolerant of fluctuating parameters and will melt at the base or drop leaves if dosing or CO2 delivery becomes inconsistent. Best placed in the midground to background as a colour-accent grouping. Ludwigia inclinata is generally regarded as one of the more striking colour-accent stem plants available for display aquascapes, but is rarely suitable for beginners given its exacting demands.
breedingNotes: Ludwigia inclinata is propagated by stem cuttings. Healthy tops are cut from a mature stem and replanted directly into the substrate; replanting the cut tops rather than the rooted base is generally recommended, as the original base often dies back after being topped. Lateral side shoots produced after topping can be trimmed and replanted in turn for mass propagation. Emersed culture — growing the plant above the waterline in a humid environment — is possible and can be a useful method for stock recovery.

primary.name: Wikipedia
primary.notes: Primary source after Buce Plant product page returned no species-specific content. Used for taxonomy (Onagraceae, L.f.) M.Gomez authority), native range (Central and South America), habitat (emersed/submersed on dried pond/lake beds), morphology (opposite leaf pairs, green-to-red gradient, wavy texture), cultivar identification (var. verticillata 'Pantanal' and 'Cuba'), and general aquarium recommendations (medium-high light, iron and micronutrient dosing, CO2 highly recommended, stem-cutting propagation).

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Secondary source for husbandry parameters on the 'Cuba' cultivar: temperature 18-30°C, pH 4-7, gH 0-30 dGH, kH 0-8 dKH, lighting high (~0.5-1 W/L), CO2 required, growth rate fast, height 15-60 cm, placement middle-to-background, propagation by cuttings (replant cut shoots to avoid die-off), nitrate 5-15 mg/L, phosphate 1-2 mg/L, iron and micronutrient supplementation required, care medium. Used to constrain the parameter ranges; final pH/gH/kH narrowed from Aquasabi's wide tolerance to the soft-acidic sweet-spot commonly cited by aquascapers for best colour.
